key_insight: LEIA is a learned world model for solid mechanics that updates deformation and stress fields step by step under applied boundary conditions, even for large 3D architected materials.
engineering_relevance: It offers real-time surrogate analysis and candidate ranking for architected material design, helping accelerate FEM-guided discovery and optimization.
method: Autoregressive world model for interactive loading and response prediction
Handles both explicit lattice microstructures and implicit homogeneous models with internal state
Works on large 3D unstructured meshes with nonlinear, history-dependent behavior
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates LEIA on MicroPlate, a benchmark spanning two microstructure regimes, and compares it against four baseline methods with finite-element ground truth for stress-accurate ranking.
watch_item: Performance may depend on benchmark-specific training data and careful handling of mesh complexity, history effects, and extrapolation to unseen loading or designs.
paper_title: LEIA: Learned Environment for Interactive Architected Materials
